history_text: 68-year-old male with a 25-year history of Type 2 diabetes mellitus, currently well-controlled (HbA1c 7.0%). Comorbidities include coronary artery disease, obesity, hypertension. Renal function is mildly to moderately reduced (eGFR 61.3 mL/min/1.73m²). Current medications include Insulin glargine 20 units nightly, Metoprolol succinate 50mg once daily, Amlodipine 5mg once daily. No known drug allergies. He denies recent hospitalisations or surgical procedures.
